subject: Re: Is there a way to erase gold beads in Warp
body:
Dear Liang,
HI, thank you very much for your reply, that certainly solved my question.
One more thing, though, during what stage does the picked mask take effect? 
For now I've got non erased, non aligned stacks created by Warp, and 
aligned .xf & .taSolution by imod, if I've already got the mask model 
picked by warp, is there something else I should do, or I just need to hit 
the reconstruction tab in warp, and warp would take care of the gold beads?
Thank you for your patient!
Best,
Linhua Tai

On Sunday, March 22, 2020 at 4:40:59 AM UTC+8, 薛亮 wrote:
>
> Hi Linhua,
>
> For tomography in Warp, when you process your images, you can enable "pick 
> particle" and select a Boxnet model to do masking, similar to single 
> particle. The "particles" coordinates picked will not be used further but 
> the regions masked can be saved and automatically erased in later 
> processing. You can try to download the pre-trained boxnet online and then 
> re-train it with your own data. The re-training protocol can be found on 
> Warp website. http://www.warpem.com/warp/?page_id=137
>
> Best,
> Liang 
>
> Linhua Tai <888...@gmail.com <javascript:>> 于2020年3月20日周五 上午6:47写道：
>
>> Dear all,
>> A silly question, I'm trying to implement Warp into my current sub tomo 
>> pipeline in order to use M refinement finally, I've managed to get to the 
>> reconstruction step now, then I realize I didn't do gold erasing.. So I'm 
>> curious, is there a way to do gold bead erasing in warp?
>> Thank you in advance!
>> Linhua Tai
>>
>> --
